_comment: Widget spec for the glyph-wheel color picker. Loaded once at startup via `include_str!` so WASM can read it. First step toward user-authored widgets — everything that defines the picker's *appearance* (glyphs, proportions, chip list, copy) lives here; the pure-function layout math stays in Rust because it depends on measured shaped-glyph advances and screen dimensions that JSON can't express.

geometry._comment: Size constants shared by the layout and render paths. The picker is now sized as a *widget* — its base font size is derived from the screen's shorter side (`target_frac * short_axis * size_scale / wheel_side_in_fonts`), then clamped to [`font_min`, `font_max`]. `size_scale` defaults to 1.0 and is mutated by the right-mouse-button drag-to-resize gesture; it lives on the picker state and resets each open. Glyph halos (`outline_*`) keep the cells legible against a transparent backdrop. `transparent_backdrop` skips the opaque rect underneath so canvas content shows through.

hint_text: Esc cancel  ·  ॐ commit  ·  click previews  ·  drag to move
